rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AILEEN DANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/28/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) What was the purpose of payment of $650.15 to the petitioner's attorneys on 05/11/2018 appearing on their invoice? 2) The petitioner's time sheets show her visits to the conservatee on 11/07/2017, 11/15/2017, 12/01/2017, and 01/03/2018, but the status report (filed 09/26/2019) does not show those visits. 3) Have the conservatee's parents paid the court-approved fees and costs to the petitioner and her attorneys? 4) Is the petitioner requesting an order compelling the conservatee's parents to pay the fees and costs sought by the subject petition? If so, does the probate court have authority to issue such an order? 5) If the conservatee's parents must pay fees of the petitioner and her attorneys, are they "fees payable from other sources" within the meaning of Probate Code § 2646? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. The petitioning party must inform all counsel, self-represented parties and all parties entitled to notice of the re-set hearing date (if applicable), the CourtCall procedure and Court Call's phone number (1-888-882-6878). A remote appearance by telephone is strongly encouraged. Parties and attorneys may arrange for a telephonic appearance by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time. The requirement for advance permission to appear by CourtCall is suspended until further notice. A CourtCall appearance may be made by mobile phone. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEANNE D. O'DONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/04/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the supplement (filed 06/30/2020) has been received by the assigned examiner. 2) A corrected inventory including appraisal of furniture, furnishings, and personal items by the referee must be filed. 3) Has the sale of the lot in Pennsylvania resulted in proceeds or other property to be administered in this proceeding? If so, a supplemental inventory and appraisal must be filed. 4) Given the current condition of the estate, when does the petitioner anticipate petitioning for final distribution? *Parties and attorneys must familiarize themselves with the 5th Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division which took effect on 06/17/2020 for the court's most updated pandemic-related requirements, procedures, and protocols. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 password: 715034 to appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Has not filed Proof of mailing of inventory and notice of how to file objections.
